fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Maine CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Maine - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity maine, how to transfer CNA license to maine, maine CNA endorsement application

board.name: Maine DHHS
board.mailing_address: 161 Capitol Street, 3rd Floor, 158 State House Station, Augusta, ME 04333-0158

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Maine.

steps.title: Create Professional Licensing Portal Account
steps.description: Navigate to the State of Maine's Professional Licensing Portal and create a new account, then log in to access the online application for Nursing Assistants.

steps.title: Submit Online Application and Fee
steps.description: Complete the online application for Nursing Assistant certification through the Professional Licensing Portal and pay the $25 application fee.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Submit fingerprints for a criminal background check as instructed during the application process. This is a mandatory requirement for certification.

steps.title: Pass Competency Exam
steps.description: Register with Prometric (Maine's approved vendor) and pass the Maine State Board of Nursing-approved Nursing Assistant Competency Examination.

steps.title: Receive Certification
steps.description: Upon successful completion of all requirements, the Maine State Board of Nursing will issue your CNA certification.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Maine CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in Maine. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Maine.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Maine?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maine while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: Information on how to become a CNA in ME.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA license to Maine.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Maine
hero.dek: To apply for your Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) certification in Maine, you must submit your application through the State of Maine's Professional Licensing Portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Maine | Registry Requirements
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to getting your CNA license in ME. Learn about the 180-hour training requirement and testing.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the State of Maine's Professional Licensing Portal, click 'New Registration' to create an account, verify your email, and log in. Once logged in, navigate to the Maine State Board of Nursing and select Nursing Assistant to start your application.
